highlights.SignedData.cs: 96% line / 92% branch (uncovered: lines 196, 200 — null-sig and bad-base64 guards)
highlights.Providers/this.cs: 83% line / 50% branch (uncovered: List(Type), Remove null-name guard, SetDefault null-name guard, ResolveType branches)
highlights.DefaultIdentityProvider.cs: 88% line / 83% branch (uncovered: Deserialize catch block lines 301, 310-312)
highlights.provider/load.cs: 28% line / 6% branch (most logic untested — only assembly-not-found path hit)

findings.issue: Entire list action has 0% coverage — no test calls list.Run()
findings.impact: All 3 code paths (null type = list all, valid type, unknown type) untested. Deletion of the entire file breaks zero tests.

findings.issue: UnknownType error path (line 19) untested. Passing Type='quantum' should return UnknownType error.
findings.impact: A PLang user writing 'remove provider x from quantum' gets an unhandled NPE instead of clean error if this guard is broken.

findings.issue: UnknownType error path (line 19) untested. Same gap as remove.
findings.impact: Same as finding #2 — NPE instead of error on invalid type.

findings.issue: All 3 catch blocks untested (lines 34-36 GenerateKeyPair, 53-55 Sign, 74-76 Verify). No test passes malformed base64 or triggers NSec exceptions.
findings.impact: If catch blocks are removed, raw exceptions propagate instead of clean Data errors. No test catches this regression.
findings.suggestion: Add tests: Sign_InvalidBase64PrivateKey_ReturnsSigningError, Verify_InvalidBase64PublicKey_ReturnsSignatureInvalid, GenerateKeyPair catch is harder to trigger but should be noted

findings.issue: SignedData.Verify() null-signature guard (line 196) and invalid-base64 catch (line 200) are uncovered. Coverage confirms 0 hits on these lines.
findings.impact: These are security guards at the crypto boundary. If removed, NullReferenceException or FormatException escapes instead of clean 'SignatureInvalid' error.

findings.issue: ResolveType() — only 'signing' branch tested (via remove/setDefault actions). Branches for 'identity', 'crypto', 'key', unknown type, and null all untested. Delete line 185-188 — no test fails.
findings.impact: If someone renames a mapping, no test catches it. Provider actions for non-signing types silently break.
findings.suggestion: Add direct tests: ResolveType_Identity, ResolveType_Crypto, ResolveType_Key, ResolveType_Unknown_ReturnsNull, ResolveType_Null_DefaultsToSigning

findings.issue: Sign_MissingIdentity_ReturnsError (line 264-266) only checks result.Success is false. Does not check Error.Key.
findings.impact: If the error key changes from the expected value, this test still passes — false green.
findings.suggestion: Add: await Assert.That(result.Error!.Key).IsEqualTo('KeyGenerationError') or whatever the expected key is

findings.issue: Sign_ProviderThrows_ReturnsDataFromError (line 277-280) checks result.Success is false and result.Error is not null, but doesn't check Error.Key.
findings.impact: Same false-green risk as #7 — any error passes this test.

findings.issue: Verify_ProviderThrows_ReturnsDataFromError (line 413-415) only checks result.Success is false. Doesn't verify Error.Key.

load action has 28% line coverage. Only the assembly-not-found path is tested. Multi-provider DLL scanning (lines 34-63), null-path guard (line 21), ProviderConstructor error (line 46) are all untested through the action.
findings.impact: The core functionality of load — scanning types, instantiating providers, registering them — has no test proving it works.
findings.suggestion: Hard to test without a real DLL. Consider creating a test helper assembly or testing the inner logic separately.

findings.issue: Non-generic Remove/SetDefault null-name guards (lines 136, 157) untested. GetOrDefault<T>() (lines 53-57) and Has<T>() (lines 96-99) untested.
findings.impact: Minor — defensive guards. But deletion of these lines breaks zero tests.

findings.issue: No PLang .goal tests exist for the provider module (load, remove, setDefault, list). Only signing and identity have PLang tests.
findings.impact: The full builder → runtime pipeline is not validated for provider module actions.
findings.suggestion: Add PLang test goals for provider list and provider setDefault at minimum

findings.issue: Settings.cs has 0% line coverage. Properties DefaultAlgorithm and DefaultTimeoutMs are never accessed in tests.
findings.impact: Low — it's a POCO with defaults. But if defaults change, no test catches it.
findings.suggestion: Add a simple test verifying default values match expected constants

summary: 1795/1803 C# tests pass (8 skipped). 15 PLang signing test goals exist (not run — no builder available). Coverage analysis reveals 6 major gaps: provider/list.cs at 0%, Ed25519Provider catch blocks at 0%, SignedData.Verify security guards uncovered, ResolveType 4/7 branches untested, UnknownType error paths in remove/setDefault untested, load.cs at 28%. 3 weak assertions check Success without Error.Key. Overall test quality is good for happy paths and core verification logic, but error paths and provider module actions have significant gaps.
